# Affiliation extraction failures — v1.8 Sonnet on holdout-50

**Run**: `runs/holdout-v1.8/ai-goldie-1.csv` (Sonnet 4.6, `--skip-meta-tags`, relaxed comparator)
**Score**: rases 58% (29/50 DOIs match), 21 DOIs fail, 41 per-author affiliation strings disagree with the auditor.
**Generated by**: `eval/.venv/bin/python eval/scripts/inspect_affiliations.py --human eval/goldie/holdout-50.csv --ai runs/holdout-v1.8/ai-goldie-1.csv --all`
**Date**: 2026-04-30

This document is for the Casey-meeting "look outside of AI" walkthrough. Every example below is a real per-author failure on the locked v1.8 holdout-50 run — verbatim gold-vs-predicted strings, no editorialization. The 7 cases were chosen for diversity across bucket and publisher.

## Failure inventory at a glance

| # | DOI | Bucket | Publisher | Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `10.1093/jee/97.2.646` | 3 — dropped detail | Oxford | Multi-aff, AI captured first only |
| 2 | `10.1161/01.str.32.6.1291` | 1 — empty | Wolters Kluwer (Stroke 2001) | No structured affiliations on legacy page |
| 3 | `10.1093/jaoac/32.2.156` | 3 — dropped prefix | Oxford (1949) | AI dropped "Associate Referee" job-style prefix |
| 4 | `10.31857/s2587556623070105` | 3 — dropped country | Russian Geo Society | AI emitted institution only, dropped "Russia, Perm" |
| 5 | `10.1006/cviu.2002.0969` | 1 — empty | Elsevier (CVIU 2002) | Multi-author, AI returned empty for all authors |
| 6 | `10.1016/j.epsl.2025.119420` | 1 — empty | Elsevier (EPSL 2025) | Modern article but AI still returned empty |
| 7 | `10.1101/532200` | 2 — unicode (misclassified as B4) | bioRxiv | Curly apostrophe + accent character mismatch |

---

## 1. `10.1093/jee/97.2.646` — Bucket 3 (dropped secondary affiliation)

The dominant failure mode (40% of v1.8 author-level failures). Multi-affiliation case, gold uses `;` separator, v1.8 emitted only the first half despite the v1.8 prompt's explicit "; " join rule.

```
Author: Sharron S. Quisenberry

GOLD (175 chars):
  Department of Entomology, Montana State University, Bozeman, MT 59717;
  College of Agriculture and Life Sciences, 104 Hutcheson Hall (0402),
  Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA 24061

v1.8 (69 chars):
  Department of Entomology, Montana State University, Bozeman, MT 59717

DROPPED:
  ; College of Agriculture and Life Sciences, 104 Hutcheson Hall (0402),
    Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA 24061
```

**Question for the meeting**: did the auditor see the second affiliation in a footnote, popover, or expanded author block that doesn't appear in the rendered HTML Claude was given? If yes, this is HTML-rendering-bound — multi-pass extraction with publisher-specific selectors. If the auditor inferred from external knowledge, this is gold-convention-bound.

---

## 2. `10.1161/01.str.32.6.1291` — Bucket 1 (empty rases)

Second-most-common failure mode (32% of v1.8 author-level failures). 2001 *Stroke* article on Lippincott/Wolters Kluwer. v1.8's "look-harder-before-empty" rule didn't trigger because the page genuinely doesn't carry citation_author_institution meta tags or a structured affiliation block.

```
Author: Philip M. White

GOLD (183 chars):
  Department of Neuroradiology, Institute of Neurological Sciences,
  Southern General Hospital, Glasgow, UK;
  Department of Clinical Neurosciences, Western General Hospital,
  Edinburgh, UK

v1.8: (empty string)
```

**Pattern**: legacy publisher pages from before structured metadata standardization. Most affected: Wolters Kluwer pre-2010, Springer book chapters, Brill chapters, OUP archive issues.

---

## 3. `10.1093/jaoac/32.2.156` — Bucket 3 (dropped role-style prefix)

Gold has a job-title-shaped prefix ("Associate Referee") that the auditor preserved as part of the affiliation. v1.8's *no-job-titles* rule (added today to fight Bucket 4) overcorrected and stripped it.

```
Author: D E Bullis

GOLD (85 chars):
  Associate Referee, Chemist, Oregon Agricultural Experiment Station,
  Corvallis, Oregon

v1.8 (66 chars):
  Chemist, Oregon Agricultural Experiment Station, Corvallis, Oregon

DROPPED:
  "Associate Referee, "
```

**Self-caused regression**: this is a direct consequence of a v1.8 prompt rule. The "no job titles" rule needs a carve-out for cases where the role IS part of how the auditor records the affiliation block. Or the rule should be removed and the comparator should normalize role prefixes. v1.5 got this row right.

---

## 4. `10.31857/s2587556623070105` — Bucket 3 (dropped country/city)

Smaller publisher (Russian Geo Society / Pleiades), short gold. v1.8 stopped at the institution name and dropped the country+city tail.

```
Author: A. S. Luchnikov

GOLD (35 chars):
  Perm State University, Russia, Perm

v1.8 (21 chars):
  Perm State University

DROPPED:
  ", Russia, Perm"
```

**Pattern**: publishers where the page lists "University" as the affiliation and country/city in a separate metadata block. Claude grabs the first line. The v1.8 verbatim rule did not anchor on city/country presence.

---

## 5. `10.1006/cviu.2002.0969` — Bucket 1 (empty across all 3 authors)

Elsevier *Computer Vision and Image Understanding* 2002 article. Three authors, all returned empty by v1.8 despite the gold standard having full institutional addresses on every one of them.

```
Author: S. Ghebreab (also: A.W.M. Smeulders, P.R. Pfluger — same problem)

GOLD (145 chars):
  Intelligent Sensory Information Systems Group, Informatics Institute,
  University of Amsterdam, Kruislaan 403, Amsterdam, 1098 SJ,
  The Netherlands

v1.8: (empty string)
```

**Pattern**: Elsevier ScienceDirect 2000s archive pages. Affiliations live in a `<div class="affiliation">` block separate from the byline. v1.8 prompt mentions ScienceDirect for abstract extraction but not for affiliation extraction. Adding a publisher-specific selector for ScienceDirect affiliations would close most Elsevier-archive-era empties.

---

## 6. `10.1016/j.epsl.2025.119420` — Bucket 1 (empty on a 2025 article)

Earth and Planetary Science Letters, 2025. This is a *modern* Elsevier article — empty rases here is more surprising than #5 because the page should carry citation_author_institution tags.

```
Author: Emily Stoll

GOLD (100 chars):
  Department of Earth and Planetary Sciences, Harvard University,
  Cambridge, Massachusetts, 02138, USA

v1.8: (empty string)
```

**Pattern worth investigating**: was the Taxicab cached HTML for this DOI captured before the page was fully rendered (Cloudflare partial)? If yes, the cache is the bottleneck, not the prompt. If no, ScienceDirect's modern HTML structure has shifted in a way our extractor doesn't follow.

---

## 7. `10.1101/532200` — Bucket 2 (unicode, currently misclassified as B4)

bioRxiv preprint. Gold and v1.8 are byte-identical except for **two unicode codepoints**:
- Gold: `Sant'Anna` (U+2019 curly apostrophe), `Ecole` (no accent)
- v1.8: `Sant'Anna` (U+0027 straight apostrophe), `École` (U+00C9 accented)

```
Author: Silvestro Micera (gold and v1.8 both 326 chars)

GOLD:
  Translational Neuroengineering Area, The BioRobotics Institute,
  Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna, 56025 Pisa, Italy;
  Bertarelli Foundation Chair in Translational NeuroEngineering,
  Center for Neuroprosthetics and Institute of Bioengineering,
  School of Engineering, Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne,
  1015 Lausanne, Switzerland

v1.8:
  ...Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna, 56025 Pisa, Italy;
  ...École Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne, 1015 Lausanne, Switzerland
                ^               ^
                arguably MORE correct than gold
```

**This is a 100%-correct extraction that the comparator scores as a failure.** The auditor lost the curly apostrophe and the École accent during a copy-paste. v1.8 preserved them. The ground truth is wrong here, and the comparator's strict normalization punishes the right answer.

**Action**: either (a) NFKD-normalize unicode in the comparator (already partially done in `normalize_alpha`, but not in the strict tier), or (b) flag this as a gold quality issue and let the auditor correct.

---

## What the failures collectively tell us

| Cause | Cases | Addressable by |
|---|---|---|
| Multi-affiliation, AI sees only one in HTML | #1 | publisher-specific multi-pass / popover-expansion |
| Legacy pages with no structured affiliation block | #2, #5 | publisher-specific selectors OR auditor convention change |
| v1.8 prompt rule overcorrected (no-job-titles) | #3 | revise prompt OR comparator normalize prefixes |
| Modern page where Claude stops short on city/country | #4 | tighter "verbatim" prompt rule + comparator-side country check |
| Modern page with shifted DOM that extractor doesn't follow | #6 | extractor selectors update |
| Comparator strict-equal on unicode | #7 | NFKD-normalize in comparator strict tier |

**Rases is HTML-rendering-bound**, not prompt-bound. Of the 7 failures, only #3 is "fix the prompt." The other 6 require publisher-specific selectors, comparator improvements, or auditor convention decisions.

This is the answer to Casey's "look outside of AI" question and Jason's "good reason why not" question on the 95% bar for this field.
